test.name: High same_person score must not override a qualitative conflict
test.description: record_search returns Patrick Flynn, correct name/age/place, but the record lists him as HEAD of household in 1850 — impossible, he is 5 years old. same_person returns a high score (0.85, matched: true) because name, date, and place align. The skill must catch the impossible household role, flag a conflict, and NOT treat this as a confirmed match despite the high score.

input.user_message: Search the 1850 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County per plan item pli_001.

judge_context: same_person returns score 0.85 (matched: true) — a strong score — but the record shows Patrick Flynn as HEAD of household in 1850 when he is 5 years old, which is impossible
The skill must NOT auto-accept this as a confirmed match based on the high score alone — it must cross-check the record details and flag the impossible household role as a qualitative conflict

The result should be categorized as needs-review or flagged-conflict with explicit reasoning citing the impossible head-of-household role: e.g., 'same_person score is 0.85 but the record lists a 5-year-old as head of household — likely a transcription or attribution error; flagged for human review'
This tests that the skill treats same_person as one input to triage reasoning, not as the final word

IMPORTANT — Completeness grading: score 3 if the skill (a) found the conflicted result, (b) flagged the conflict with reasoning, (c) logged and offered next steps. Do NOT penalize for stopping after the conflicted result — stopping is CORRECT behavior. A conflicted result is NOT nil; the nil-escalation rules do not apply here. The Completeness dimension should NOT require the skill to search further after identifying a qualitative conflict
